patterns.name: Byte-identical client ShutDown() across KV/Object/Stream clients
patterns.rationale: KVClient::ShutDown, ObjectClient::ShutDown and StreamClient::ShutDown are byte-for-byte identical: same TraceGuard setup, same impl_ null-check, same ShutDown/CompleteHandler sequence and OK fallback. The recovery-critical shutdown path is copy-pasted three times, so any fix (e.g. changing rollback handling) must be applied in three places or drift silently. Extract a shared helper (e.g. a base-class ShutDownImpl or free function taking impl_).

patterns.name: Byte-identical client Init() in KV and Object clients
patterns.rationale: KVClient::Init and ObjectClient::Init are byte-for-byte identical, including the (void)metrics::InitKvMetrics() call, needRollbackState flag, impl_->Init(needRollbackState, true) and CompleteHandler sequence. This init/recovery sequence is duplicated verbatim; a shared helper would keep the two clients' startup semantics in lockstep.

patterns.name: Near-identical AccessRecorder wrapper in ObjectClient GIncreaseRef/GDecreaseRef
patterns.rationale: ObjectClient::GIncreaseRef and ObjectClient::GDecreaseRef differ only in the impl_ method name and the order of two RequestParam field assignments; the TraceGuard/AccessRecorder/RequestParam/Record boilerplate is otherwise copy-pasted. The same record-and-return wrapper repeats across most ObjectClient methods, making it a strong candidate for a single templated/helper wrapper.
